evals.name: paper-to-beamer-conference
evals.prompt: Turn this paper into a Beamer deck for a 15-minute conference talk. Give me the actual .tex.
evals.expected_behavior: Triggers beamer-academic-deck. First produces a per-slide spec with claim-titles and ~1 slide/min pacing, confirms, then emits compile-ready .tex using the metropolis preamble with marked figure placeholders and \note speaker notes.

evals.name: lecture-slides
evals.prompt: I'm teaching a 75-minute lecture on diffusion models. Build me Beamer slides with worked examples.
evals.expected_behavior: Triggers beamer-academic-deck in lecture mode: section recaps, worked examples, larger fonts, tolerable redundancy; spec first, then .tex.

evals.name: disambig-vs-talk-planner
evals.prompt: How long should each section of my conference talk be and what should I cut?
evals.expected_behavior: Defers to research-talk-planner (narrative/pacing/what-to-cut), not beamer-academic-deck, since no Beamer artifact is requested yet.

evals.name: disambig-vs-proposal
evals.prompt: Build Beamer slides for my PhD proposal defense with a timeline and milestones.
evals.expected_behavior: Defers to beamer-proposal-report-deck (plan-of-work spine: objectives, Gantt, milestones, risks), not beamer-academic-deck.

evals.name: content-reasoning-method-slides
evals.prompt: Build lecture slides in Beamer explaining the MDCT and why audio codecs use it.
evals.expected_behavior: Triggers beamer-academic-deck (lecture mode) and reasons about content with professor-mentor-technical-teaching: names the pressure (blocking artifacts vs. compactness), puts intuition before the transform equations, earns each equation, and carries the fundamental tradeoff. Compresses to one signal per slide with depth in \note/backup — does NOT expand the eight-stage teaching arc into eight dense slides.
